scope: Scenario-aware calibration matrix for the single primary FX micromarket model.
ingestion_method: Semi-automated pypdf extraction plus hand-curated scale-matched proxies where the available literature is not directly in model units.

calibration_scenarios.name: baseline_primary
calibration_scenarios.description: Primary baseline without explicit exogenous shock.

calibration_scenarios.name: dealer_liquidity_crisis
calibration_scenarios.description: Dealer-balance-sheet crisis used for withdrawal and resiliency targets.

calibration_scenarios.name: funding_liquidity_shock
calibration_scenarios.description: Funding-liquidity stress scenario for propagation metrics.

targets.market_scope: EUR/USD major calm baseline / ECN-style FX market
targets.statistic: median quoted spread in normal conditions
targets.source_excerpt: EUR/USD is found to be the most liquid exchange rate ... The raw spreads have been converted into basis points by multiplication with 10,000.
targets.ingestion_note: Acceptance now uses the median, not the mean, quoted spread. For a calm EUR/USD major-pair baseline the honest anchor is a tight 1.5-2.5 bps band; the earlier 4.2 bps point target was too historical and too loose for this use-case.

targets.market_scope: dealer-dominated limit-order-book depth at the best quotes
targets.statistic: one-sided touch depth at best bid/ask
targets.source_excerpt: Table 2 reports L1 bid volumes for large names such as 6.360, 5.654, 1.793 and 0.952 (x1000 shares).
targets.ingestion_note: The target now maps the literature's L1 touch depth into simulator units directly at the best quotes rather than using the broader 25 bps near-mid band.

targets.market_scope: EUR/USD high-trading-density signed order flow and CLOB-facing persistence
targets.statistic: lag-1 autocorrelation of non-zero signed CLOB-side order flow
targets.source_excerpt: Table D.1 ... EUR/USD ... the statistics in column (ii) test for the presence of (residual) serial correlation in the price changes, order flow and bid-ask spread process.
targets.ingestion_note: Switched from raw CLOB-flow imbalance rho to signed non-zero CLOB flow rho. The new band enforces small-but-material positive serial dependence consistent with high-trading-density EUR/USD rather than the old 0.10 ± 0.10 convenience proxy.

targets.statistic: mean run length of non-zero signed CLOB-side order flow
targets.source_excerpt: Table D.1 documents non-zero EUR/USD order-flow serial dependence in high trading density windows, motivating short same-sign run clustering rather than iid flow signs.
targets.ingestion_note: Diagnostic only: this companion metric checks short parent-order clustering so the calibration does not rely exclusively on lag-1 autocorrelation. The band is deliberately modest and rules out both iid signs and unrealistically long toxic meta-order runs.

targets.statistic: impact of a medium trade bucket expressed in model units
targets.source_excerpt: The average magnitude of the significant c1 corresponding to a price impact roughly ranges from 20 to 72 basis points per $1 billion in the order flow.
targets.ingestion_note: Disabled as an objective term for now because the available paper is not directly in simulator trade-size units.

targets.source_excerpt: The spread is found to be resilient ... the effect has largely dissipated after 100 order events ... median half-life estimates range between 1 and 3 seconds.
targets.ingestion_note: The simulator tick aggregates multiple order events, so the literature event-time recovery is mapped to a 20-tick coarse half-life target.

targets.statistic: share of dealers withdrawn during the shock window
targets.source_excerpt: At times when dealers' intermediation capacity is constrained the cost of liquidity provision increases disproportionately relative to dealer-intermediated volume.
targets.ingestion_note: The literature is qualitative on dealer exits, so the target is a cautious stress-window withdrawal-share proxy rather than a direct estimate.

targets.market_scope: funding stress propagation to spreads and depth
targets.statistic: correlation proxy between funding stress and quoted spread
targets.source_excerpt: Systematic and currency-specific liquidity risk explain up to 40% of the time series variation in carry trade premia.
targets.ingestion_note: The acceptance check uses a normalized funding-liquidity propagation proxy rather than a structural elasticity estimated directly from dealer panels.

targets.source_excerpt: The coexistence literature motivates a tight but non-zero cross-venue price wedge; the extracted JRFM survey is descriptive rather than directly calibrated.
targets.ingestion_note: A 15 bps basis anchor better matches the current model's coarse FX-AMM coexistence scale while remaining well inside the 25 bps sanity ceiling.

targets.market_scope: share of routed flow executed on AMMs
targets.statistic: average AMM volume share in coexistence equilibrium
targets.source_excerpt: Despite this restricted strategy space for liquidity provision, AMMs have experienced significant growth in trading share since 2020.
targets.ingestion_note: The paper confirms meaningful coexistence but the extracted text does not provide a stable point estimate, so the fitter uses a 22% AMM-share proxy.

targets.statistic: reserve scale, fee bands, and basis persistence plausibility
targets.source_excerpt: Trading in the FX market reached an all-time high of $5.3 trillion per day in April 2013 ... spot trading almost reached the same volume as FX swaps at $2.05 trillion per day.
targets.ingestion_note: The FX turnover survey is used only as a coarse plausibility anchor for pool scale and fee bounds, not as a direct AMM calibration source.
